items.front: When may departure information from ATIS be omitted?
items.back: When the pilot states the appropriate ATIS code.

items.front: When must a departure delay be communicated to the pilot?
What departure control instructions must be issued?
items.back: Initial heading, altitude, and (when in an LOA) departure frequency and beacon code.

items.front: What is LUAW and what is its time limitation?
items.back: Line Up and Wait. Do not authorize if the aircraft has been holding for more than the time specified in the facility directive without additional information being provided.
Issuing a takeoff clearance when separation will exist at the time the departure begins its takeoff roll, even if the preceding aircraft is still on the runway.

items.front: What is the basic same-runway departure separation requirement?
items.back: The departing aircraft may not begin takeoff roll until the preceding aircraft has crossed the runway end, turned to avoid conflict, or is airborne.

items.front: What additional separation applies to intersection departures?
items.back: Wake turbulence separation requires additional time or distance when a smaller aircraft departs from an intersection behind a larger aircraft that used the full length.

items.front: What must a controller ensure for intersecting runway departures?
items.back: Separation from aircraft operating on the intersecting runway.

items.front: What is the key concern for nonintersecting converging runway operations?
items.back: Ensuring aircraft flight paths do not intersect at the same time in the area where they converge.

items.front: When is the takeoff clearance issued?
items.back: Once the aircraft is in position and takeoff separation has been applied.

items.front: What is the proper procedure for canceling a takeoff clearance?
items.back: Transmit the aircraft identification, state CANCEL TAKEOFF CLEARANCE, and provide the reason.
